ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento class; no specific fish fillet and teriyaki chicken bento entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers cooked fish fillet as the primary protein component. No composite bento code exists; fish fillet component used as primary anchor. Teriyaki chicken component is a secondary protein not separately coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for this composite dish found in publicly available SNOMED CT browser as of 2026.

serving.description: 1 bento box (protein components only, excluding rice)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of this bento dish is approximately 200 g of the combined fish fillet and teriyaki chicken protein components, as commonly portioned in Singaporean hawker and food court bento boxes.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Fillet + Teriyaki Chicken Bento (多利鱼+鸡扒饭盒)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for this dish (cr_id G00010135). All per_100g values are sourced from HPB with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 1.8 g/100g based on the known fatty acid profile of dory fish and chicken thigh in a teriyaki preparation; this field is not directly reported in the HPB entry and is documented as an approximation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed protein-dominant dishes — analogous estimate based on low-carbohydrate protein foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: This dish contains only 1.62 g carbohydrate per 100g. No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. A GI of 45 (low category) is assigned as a conservative analogous estimate for a high-protein, low-carbohydrate cooked fish and chicken dish with teriyaki glaze, consistent with GI values reported for similar protein-dominant dishes with small amounts of sugar-containing sauce in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving = (45 × 1.62 / 100) × 200g / 100 = 0.7, which is clinically negligible. The practical glycaemic impact of this dish is minimal when consumed without the rice component.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 723 mg/100g — exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish in its standard preparation; if consumed, request teriyaki sauce on the side or a reduced-sodium version, and restrict portion to no more than 100 g to keep sodium intake below 750 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.62 g/100g) and sugar (1.47 g/100g) with high protein (25.73 g/100g) supports glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: The protein components of this bento are suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to negligible carbohydrate content; however, if served with white rice as part of the full bento, the rice portion substantially raises glycaemic load and patients should request brown rice or reduce the rice portion accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (723 mg/100g) is the primary cardiovascular concern; fat profile is moderate with estimated saturated fat ~1.8 g/100g and cholesterol 105 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lean protein content and moderate fat profile are broadly acceptable for cardiovascular health, but the high sodium load from teriyaki and soy-based marinade poses a meaningful risk for blood pressure elevation; individuals with CVD should limit portion size, request reduced-sodium sauce, and account for this dish's sodium contribution within a daily limit of 2000 mg.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (723 mg/100g) may exacerbate tissue oedema; saturated fat is low (~1.8 g/100g) which is favourable
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Dory fish provides lean protein and modest omega-3 fatty acids while chicken is a lower arachidonic acid white meat; however, the high sodium content from teriyaki sauce may worsen RA-related tissue swelling and patients should request sauce on the side or a reduced-sodium preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Fillet and Teriyaki Chicken Bento (多利鱼+鸡扒饭盒) is a popular Singaporean food court and hawker bento dish combining two protein components: a pan-fried or shallow-fried dory fish fillet (Pangasius hypophthalmus) and a grilled or pan-seared chicken piece glazed with teriyaki sauce. The teriyaki glaze is a Japanese-origin sweet-savoury sauce made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar, which imparts a caramelised, umami-rich coating to the chicken. The dory fillet is typically lightly seasoned and fried until golden. Both proteins are served together in a bento-style box, commonly accompanied by steamed white rice and simple vegetable sides, though the nutritional data here covers the protein components only. The dish has a savoury, slightly sweet flavour profile with a tender, moist texture from the fish and a firmer, glazed texture from the chicken. It is a high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish per 100g (protein 25.73 g, carbohydrate 1.62 g, fat 5.96 g, energy 163 kcal), making it suitable for glycaemic management when consumed without the rice component. The primary clinical concern is the high sodium content of 723 mg/100g derived from the teriyaki and soy-based marinade, which makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease unless modified. Cholesterol is 105 mg/100g, which is moderate. This dish is widely available in Singaporean hawker centres, food courts, and Japanese-style fast food outlets, and is a common lunch and dinner option across all ethnic communities in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id G00010135) with confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat is the only estimated field, documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database protocol, documented with full rationale.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100g) is estimated based on the known fatty acid profile of dory fish and chicken thigh in a teriyaki preparation; this value is not directly reported in the HPB source entry and should be updated when HPB publishes detailed fatty acid data for this dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is a confirmed zero from the approved source reflecting the absence of significant fibre in the protein components of this dish. 4. Glycemic index is set to 45 (low category) as a conservative analogous estimate for this high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish (1.62 g carbohydrate per 100g); no direct GI measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for this composite dish. Glycemic load per serving is 0.7, which is clinically negligible. 5. The bento dish as sold in Singaporean food courts typically includes white rice; the nutritional data here covers the protein components only as recorded by HPB. 6. Halal status is marked true as dory fish and chicken are permissible under halal dietary law when sourced and prepared appropriately; consumers should verify halal certification with the specific vendor.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' for FoodOn and FoodEx2 per G5 hard gate requirement.

Iteration 3: Fixed q4 failure — changed glycemic_index from null to 45 (numeric, low category) as a conservative analogous estimate for this high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish, per validator feedback that glycemic_index must be numeric. Updated gi_category from 'not_applicable' to 'low'. Updated glycemic_load_per_serving to 0.7. Updated gi_source to document analogous estimate basis. Updated notes to reflect GI analogous estimate rationale.
